source: Ash feature request 2026-06-21 â€” before any guest execution, permission is taken from Ash.
title: Profiles: execution approval workflow (human-in-the-loop)
current_state: No approval mechanism exists. T-224 routes guest execution tools to a 'pending approval' stub; this ticket implements the actual request/approve/deny lifecycle.
target_state: When a guest action needs execution, a pending-approval record is created with an unguessable token (secrets.token_urlsafe), the requesting profile, the tool + args summary, and a TTL (e.g. 30 min). Ash is notified on his Telegram with the summary and the token. Ash approves via '/approve <token>' or denies via '/deny <token>' â€” accepted ONLY from Ash's auth (chat_id==TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID). On approval the queued tool runs in the guest's own namespace and the result is delivered back to the guest's chat; on deny or expiry it is dropped and the guest is told. Pending requests are listable by Ash ('/approvals').
migration_plan: 1. Approvals store: 'approvals' table (token, profile_name, tool, args_json, status[pending|approved|denied|expired], created_at, expires_at) in the default pi.db (Ash-owned control plane).

2. request_execution_approval(profile, tool, args) -> creates a record + token, sends Ash a Telegram message via send_message (summary + token), returns a user-facing 'waiting for Ash's approval' string.

3. /approve <token> & /deny <token> & /approvals handlers in tools_telegram, ash-only (reuse the resolve-profile==ash check). On approve: load record, re-dispatch the tool in the guest's namespace (profile_switch), deliver result to the guest's chat_id; mark approved. Expiry checked lazily on access + skipped if past expires_at.
4. Safety: token is single-use; re-approving an already-resolved token is a no-op with a clear message. Args are summarized/truncated in the Ash notification (no secret leakage).

5. Tests: testing/test_execution_approval.py â€” guest exec creates a pending record + notifies ash (mock send); /approve by ash runs the tool in the guest namespace and returns result; /approve by a guest is refused; expired token cannot be approved; denied token never executes; token is single-use.
risk_notes: Control-plane tables (profiles, approvals) live in Ash's pi.db, never in a guest DB â€” a guest must not be able to read or mutate them. Keep the notification args summarized. This ticket is the trust hinge; test the negative paths (guest approving, expired, double-approve) as hard as the happy path.
